whatItDoes: Following the rules of logic to reach a conclusion.
Estimating how likely something is, given prior evidence.
Parsing words. Building sentences. Tracking grammar.
whatLightsUp: A network spanning the left frontal & temporal lobes.

whatItDoes: Modelling other minds. Following stories. Making meaning.
whatLightsUp: A wide network across both sides of the brain and along the midline.

checkable.text: No — there is no external check.

deltas.m1->m2: Now in the right hemisphere — same kind of cortex, opposite side.
deltas.m2->m1: Back to the left hemisphere — the kind of thinking that has to be exact.
deltas.m1->m3: A different left-side network: the language regions, sitting inside the broader task-positive territory.
deltas.m3->m1: Back to the broader task-control regions that surround language.
deltas.m2->m3: Crossed back to the left hemisphere — and into a network for words, not numbers.
deltas.m3->m2: Crossed to the right hemisphere — leaving language for likelihood.
deltas.m1->m4: A wider, distributed network across both sides and the midline. The only mode without an external check.
deltas.m4->m1: Back to a single hemisphere and a checkable kind of thinking.
deltas.m2->m4: From a single right-hemisphere network to a wide distributed one — including the parts you can only see from the inside surface.
deltas.m4->m2: Back to a single right-side network with verifiable outputs.
deltas.m3->m4: Still includes the language territory, but now stretches into both hemispheres and along the midline — and checks against nothing.
deltas.m4->m3: Narrows from a brain-wide network back to the language regions in the left hemisphere.

overlaps.m1+m3: Modes 01 and 03 share territory in the left frontal cortex — the language regions sit inside the broader task-control area.
overlaps.m1+m4: Modes 01 and 04 share territory in the left parietal cortex — the angular gyrus is part of both networks.
overlaps.m2+m4: Modes 02 and 04 share territory in the right parietal cortex — including the right TPJ.
overlaps.m3+m4: Modes 03 and 04 share posterior temporal territory — the language and social-narrative networks live next to each other in the left hemisphere.
overlaps.m1+m2: Modes 01 and 02 are mirror images of each other — the same kind of frontal-parietal control, on opposite sides.
overlaps.m2+m3: Modes 02 and 03 use opposite hemispheres — Mode 02 right, Mode 03 left.
